title: Box Breathing
description: A simple breathing pattern to calm the nervous system.

steps.instruction: Find a comfortable position and gently close your eyes.
Inhale slowly through your nose.
Hold your breath gently.
Exhale slowly through your mouth.
Hold at the bottom, empty and still.
Inhale slowly through your nose.
Hold your breath gently.
Exhale slowly through your mouth.
Hold at the bottom, empty and still.
Inhale slowly through your nose.
Hold your breath gently.
Exhale slowly through your mouth.
Hold at the bottom, empty and still.
Let your breath return to its natural rhythm. Notice how you feel.

title: 4-7-8 Breathing
description: A calming breath pattern that encourages deep rest.

steps.instruction: Sit comfortably with your back supported. Let your shoulders drop.
Exhale completely through your mouth.
Inhale quietly through your nose.
Hold your breath — let your body be still.
Exhale completely through your mouth.
Inhale quietly through your nose.
Hold your breath — let your body be still.
Exhale completely through your mouth.
Inhale quietly through your nose.
Hold your breath — let your body be still.
Exhale completely through your mouth.
Return to natural breathing. Rest here for a moment.

title: Body Scan
description: A slow, gentle scan from head to toe to release held tension.

steps.instruction: Lie down or sit comfortably. Close your eyes and take a slow breath.
Bring your attention to the top of your head. Notice any sensation — warmth, tingling, tension — without judgment.
Soften your forehead, your eyes, your jaw. Let your face relax completely.
Move your awareness to your neck and shoulders. Breathe into any tightness you find there.
Notice your chest and the slow rise and fall of each breath.
Bring attention to your arms, your hands, the tips of your fingers.
Move down to your belly. Notice how it expands and contracts.
Bring awareness to your lower back and hips. Let them feel heavy and supported.
Move your attention to your legs — thighs, knees, calves.
Notice your feet and toes. Feel the weight of your whole body, grounded and at rest.
Take a full breath in and let it go. Gently bring your awareness back to the room.

title: 5-4-3-2-1 Senses
description: Anchor yourself to the present moment through each of your senses.

steps.instruction: Take a slow breath and let your eyes soften. You are here, right now.
Notice 5 things you can see. Look around slowly — shapes, colors, light.
Notice 4 things you can physically feel. The weight of your body, the texture of your clothing.
Notice 3 things you can hear. Let sounds come to you without searching.
Notice 2 things you can smell. Even faint or familiar scents.
Notice 1 thing you can taste. Whatever is present on your tongue right now.
Take a slow breath. You are present. You are here.

title: Gentle Movement
description: Simple, unhurried movements to reconnect with your body.

steps.instruction: Sit or stand comfortably. Take a breath and arrive here.
Slowly roll your shoulders back, three times. Move without rushing.
Gently tilt your right ear toward your right shoulder. Hold and breathe.
Bring your head back to center, then tilt left. Hold and breathe.
Interlace your fingers and stretch your arms overhead. Breathe into the length.
Let your arms float down. Roll your wrists slowly in both directions.
Place both feet flat on the floor. Press them down gently and feel the ground.
Take three slow breaths. Notice how your body feels now compared to when you started.

title: Safe Place Visualization
description: Guided imagery to find calm within a place that feels wholly your own.

steps.instruction: Close your eyes and take three slow, deep breaths.
Begin to imagine a place where you feel completely safe and at ease. It can be real or imagined.
Notice what you see there. The colors, the light, the shapes around you.
What sounds are present in this place? Let them settle around you.
What does the air feel like? The temperature, any breeze or stillness.
Notice how your body feels in this place. Let yourself rest here fully.
Know that this place is always available to you. You can return whenever you need.
Begin to gently bring your awareness back. Wiggle your fingers. Take a slow breath.
Open your eyes when you're ready. Carry this feeling with you.
